questions.question_text: In this Paris high school admissions process, suppose your academic grade score is 12.5, and last year's admission cutoff for School A was 14.0. If you list School A as your top choice, what is most likely to happen?
question_options: You will definitely be admitted to School A since it is your top choice
You are unlikely to be admitted to School A, because students with higher scores than yours will take the available seats first
Listing School A first guarantees you a spot if there are any empty seats
Your chance at School A is equal to every other applicant regardless of score
You will be assigned School A only if no other students list it

questions.question_text:
{{ context }}

Please provide your rank-order list of schools from most preferred (#1) to least preferred. You may list between 1 and 8 schools out of the 11 available. Write your answer as a comma-separated list of school numbers in order from most to least preferred. Example: '3, 1, 7, 5' means you prefer School 3 most, then School 1, then School 7, then School 5.
